vulnerability.title: A remote time server for Chrony is configured
vulnerability.description: Chrony is a daemon which implements the Network Time Protocol (NTP). It is designed to synchronize system clocks across a variety of systems and use a source that is highly accurate. More information on chrony can be found at https://chrony-project.org/. Chrony can be configured to be a client and/or a server. Add or edit server or pool lines to /etc/chrony.conf as appropriate: server <remote-server> Multiple servers may be configured.
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core && { rpm --quiet -q chrony; }; then var_multiple_time_servers='' config_file="/etc/chrony.conf" if ! grep -q '^[[:space:]]*\(server\|pool\)[[:space:]]\+[[:graph:]]\+' "$config_file" ; then if ! grep -q '#[[:space:]]*server' "$config_file" ; then for server in $(echo "$var_multiple_time_servers" | tr ',' '\n') ; do printf '\nserver %s' "$server" >> "$config_file" done else sed -i 's/#[ \t]*server/server/g' "$config_file" fi if [[ -s "$config_file" ]] && [[ -n "$(tail -c 1 -- "$config_file" || true)" ]]; then LC_ALL=C sed -i --follow-symlinks '$a'\\ "$config_file" fi fi else >&2 echo 'Remediation is not applicable, nothing was done' fi

triage_decision.reason: The finding merely indicates that a remote NTP server is configured for chrony, which does not affect authentication or critical boot processes. Adjusting the server list can be done safely by editing /etc/chrony.conf and restarting the service.

previous_attempts.scan_output: Single-rule scan failed for openscap_063 (rule=xccdf_org.ssgproject.content_rule_chronyd_specify_remote_server). Could not verify.

review_feedback: Review+QA approved but the verification scan failed. Try a different approach.

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: The remediation adds a remote server entry to /etc/chrony.conf but does not handle multiple servers robustly, lacks clear variable definition, and does not restart the chronyd service after modification.
concerns: Variable var_multiple_time_servers is not defined before use
Pattern matching for existing server lines could miss commented entries
Appending without checking for duplicate entries may lead to clutter on repeated runs
Hard-coded server may not suit all network policies
Does not restart chronyd to apply changes
May not preserve SELinux context on file modification
suggested_improvements: Define TIME_SERVERS explicitly or accept it as input
Use a more precise grep pattern to detect any server configuration
Add checks to avoid duplicate entries
Allow customization of server list via configuration
Restart or reload chronyd after editing the file
Run restorecon to correct SELinux context
